$description: Composition patterns for transitions — how elements arrive, depart, and transform. Every transition in the ecosystem serves one of three purposes: responsive feedback, content revelation, or spiritual pacing. Decorative transitions are forbidden (PRI-08).

responsive-feedback.$description: The portal acknowledges the seeker's action. Instant or near-instant.

content-revelation.$description: New content appears or existing content transforms. Smooth but purposeful.

spiritual-pacing.$description: Moments where the transition IS the experience — not overhead between states, but a contemplative breath in its own right.

page-transitions.$implementation: CSS View Transitions API. Two mechanisms: @view-transition { navigation: auto } for MPA navigations, Next.js experimental.viewTransition for SPA (client-side) navigations. Both use the same ::view-transition-old(root) / ::view-transition-new(root) cross-fade. Uniform contemplative pace for all navigations — no per-element view-transition-names, no tiered timing.
page-transitions.$rationale: Every page turn is a threshold crossing. The portal breathes between pages at a single, unhurried pace. Departures are swift (the seeker has already decided to leave). Arrivals are contemplative (the new teaching unfolds). Asymmetric timing: 300ms out, 800ms in.

all-navigations.$description: All page navigations share the same contemplative cross-fade. Books → book landing, book landing → chapter, chapter → chapter, home → search — all identical. The portal does not distinguish 'important' navigations from 'ordinary' ones. Every page deserves a breath.
all-navigations.mechanism: ::view-transition-old(root) and ::view-transition-new(root) with custom @keyframes. No per-element view-transition-names needed.

page-transitions.reduced-motion: all view transition animations disabled via @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce). Pages swap instantly. No fallback animation.

within-page.$description: State changes on the same page — filters, sorts, panel reveals.
within-page.approach: CSS transitions on the changing elements. Never animate layout shifts.

appear.$description: An element becoming visible (dropdown, panel, modal).
appear.transform: none (no slide-in, no scale-up — clean opacity fade)
appear.note: Slide-in is acceptable for mobile drawers (transform: translateX). Never use scale or bounce.

disappear.duration: interaction (150ms) — faster than appear. Disappearing should be quick; appearing should be noticed.
disappear.note: Asymmetric timing is intentional. Arrivals are celebrated; departures are swift.

hover.properties: background-color, border-color, color only. Never animate size, position, or shadow on hover.
hover.touch: hover effects are suppressed on touch devices (@media (hover: none))

focus.duration: instant (0ms) — focus indicators must appear immediately for accessibility
focus.style: 2px solid gold, 2px offset. No transition on outline.
focus.note: :focus-visible only (not :focus) to avoid showing outlines on mouse clicks.

theme-transitions.$description: When the seeker switches color themes. The entire page transforms. This is a content revelation — smooth but not slow.
theme-transitions.properties: background-color, color, border-color. All CSS custom properties transition simultaneously.
theme-transitions.reduced-motion: instant — no transition when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce

loading-states.$description: How the portal communicates 'waiting' without violating calm technology principles.
loading-states.approach: minimal indication that something is happening. No skeleton screens, no spinning circles, no progress bars (except for actual file operations).

search.indicator: subtle gold pulse on the search input border. 1 cycle, not looping.
search.fallback: if search takes > 3s, show a calm text message: 'Searching the teachings...'

page.indicator: none for cached/fast loads. For slow loads (> 1s), show the scroll indicator bar at 50% as a gentle hint.
page.never: never a full-screen loader, never a spinning animation, never a skeleton screen

stagger-patterns.$description: When multiple elements appear together (search results, suggestion list), they can stagger for a sense of natural arrival. Like books being placed on a shelf one at a time.
stagger-patterns.beyond-max: all remaining items appear simultaneously (don't keep the seeker waiting)
